intro: Ophiorrhiza exigua, also known as the 'Narrow-leafed Ophiorrhiza', is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Ophiorrhiza exigua include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ophiorrhiza exigua is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of tinctures and capsules, as well as being incorporated into herbal teas and infusions.
